name: Shangri La
tagline: Doris Duke's oceanfront Islamic art mansion — a jaw-dropping private estate with 2,500 pieces of art from the Islamic world.
parking: Tours depart from Honolulu Museum of Art; shuttle provided
hours: Wed-Sat; tours at set times (must pre-book)
bestTime: Book well in advance; tours are small and sell out
talkStory: Tobacco heiress Doris Duke built this stunning oceanfront estate in the 1930s and filled it with over 2,500 pieces of Islamic art collected during her world travels. The house itself is a work of art — intricate tilework, carved wooden screens, and gardens inspired by Mughal and Moorish design. Tours are small (12 people) and depart from the Honolulu Museum of Art. One of the most unique cultural experiences in all of Hawaii.
